title: Loneliness
lead: Loneliness is the gap between the connection you have and the connection you long for. It's painful, it's common, and it can ease — often in smaller steps than you'd think.
explanation: Loneliness is the distress of feeling disconnected. It isn't the same as being alone — you can feel lonely in a crowd or in a relationship, and you can feel content in solitude. It's about whether you feel seen, understood, and belonging.

It's more common than almost anyone admits. Public health leaders now treat loneliness as a serious issue affecting millions, with real effects on mood and physical health. If you feel this way, you are genuinely not alone in it.

Loneliness can become self-reinforcing: it makes us expect rejection, so we withdraw, which deepens the isolation. Gently interrupting that loop — with small, low-stakes moments of connection — is how the pattern starts to shift.
symptoms: Believing no one really understands you
Spending a lot of time on screens to fill the gap

immediate.title: One small point of contact
immediate.description: Connection doesn't have to be deep to help. Say hello to a neighbour, message an old friend, or chat with someone at a shop. Small moments of being seen add up.

immediate.title: Reach toward, not away
steps: Choose one tiny approach instead — a text, a call, a comment

immediate.title: Be kind to the lonely feeling
immediate.description: Instead of judging yourself for feeling lonely, treat it as information: it means connection matters to you. That's human, not shameful.

longTerm.title: Build around shared activity
longTerm.description: Deep friendships often grow sideways — through repeated shared activity (a class, a volunteering role, a group) rather than trying to force closeness directly.

longTerm.title: Deepen what you already have
longTerm.description: Sometimes the answer isn't more people but more depth: being a little more open and vulnerable with someone already in your life.

longTerm.title: Talk to someone
longTerm.description: Free listening services and peer communities exist precisely for this. Speaking to a trained listener can bridge the gap while you rebuild connection.

resources.title: Our Epidemic of Loneliness and Isolation
resources.whyHere: Landmark public-health report showing how common and serious loneliness is.

resources.title: Loneliness — how to cope
resources.whyHere: Warm, practical, free guidance from a leading mental health charity.

resources.title: 7 Cups
resources.whyHere: Free, anonymous listeners and community for exactly this feeling, 24/7.

resources.title: Meetup
resources.whyHere: Find free local groups built around shared interests — a gentle path to connection.

matchPhrases: no one would notice if i disappeared
i feel alone even around people
